name: Giant Chairs of Weil am Rhein

name: Kloster Kamp
description: This Cistercian monastery was the first in the German-speaking world

name: Memorial for Victims of the KZ-Außenlager Stoffeln
description: In the middle of a wonderful recreational park, this memorial reminds us of the dark past of this place.

name: Japanese Bonsai Garden
description: Tucked away on the edge of a forest, this tranquil oasis brings a piece of Japan to rural Brandenburg.

name: Apfelweibla
description: This brass doorknob is a nod to a local literary figure.

name: The Parliament of Trees
description: A curious memorial to the Berlin Wall, German division, the war, and those who died at the wall.

name: Robert Bunsen Statue
description: A larger-than-life monument to the inventor of the Bunsen burner.

name: Cecilienhof Palace
description: One of the few Tudor palaces in Germany, which hosted a historic WWII meeting.

name: The Swallowed Urn
description: Where a hospital garden hides a haunting reminder of life’s cycle.

name: Shakespeare’s Death Mask
description: A library in Germany displays a likeness that some believe is William Shakespeare.

name: Bergbahn Maschinenraum
description: A unique look into the bowels of a century-old funicular.

name: Singer Clock Tower
description: This striking clock tower was once part of the Singer sewing machine factory.

name: ‘Pineapple! The Queen of Fruits in Muskau Park’
description: This permanent exhibition showcases the historical significance and cultivation of pineapples.

name: Bernd Rosemeyer Memorial
description: A memorial for a racecar driver who died while trying to set a land speed record on the autobahn.

name: Niederwald Monument
description: Hilltop monument to the victory of a new nation in the Franco-Prussian War.

name: Kapelle 'Madonna in den Trümmern' ('Madonna in the Ruins' Chapel)
description: This little jewel of post-war religious architecture is hidden inside the Diocesan Museum, which swallowed it when it was built.

name: Waldmops of Brandenburg
description: Pugs with antlers are found all over the city as a homage to one of its most famous cartoonists.

name: Rundkappelenplatz
description: This modest German chapel has been in use for more than 1000 years.

name: Neue Wache
description: A poignant memorial that traces two hundred years of German history.

name: Powerline Eberdingen-Riet
description: 20kV overhead line with a garland-like cable.

name: Boros Collection
description: Art and concrete merge into a single entity within a WWII bunker that possesses a deeply storied and turbulent history.

name: Olbernhauer Reiterlein (Little Rider of Olbernhau)
description: Created in 1935 as a small gift for those who donated to a charity drive, this little wooden soldier became the symbol of a German town.

name: Westwall Museum
description: This museum in the small German city offers a unique glimpse into World War II history.

name: The Pink Tube
description: An intersection between architecture, industrial construction, machine, and scientific instrument.

name: Garching Maypole
description: An enormous hand-carved and painted pole.

name: Statue Stephanie de Beauharnais
description: This easily overlooked statue depicts an important woman in Mannheim's history.

name: Former Sebaldushof Factory
description: A preserved Nazi munitions factory hidden in the forest, now a quiet memorial to those who once labored and suffered there.

name: Mäusebunker
description: Along the banks of the Teltow Canal in southwestern Berlin stands one of the city’s most controversial buildings, a hulking Brutalist laboratory that looks more like a warship than a research facility.

name: Falkenburg
description: The ruins of a medieval castle that survived two sieges.

name: Bent Metal on Two Cars
description: Likely the world’s only place where you’re meant to skate on top of cars.

name: Gececondu of Kreuzberg
description: An Anatolian immigrant created his own little paradise in the shadow of the Berlin Wall, made possible by the tensions of the Cold War.

name: 'Nach Vorn'
description: A bizarre representation of our ever forward moving society.

name: Zwinger
description: A 16th-century fortress that was a Nazi-era prison and execution site, now a silent memorial.

name: The Cheeky Boy at Hummel Fountain
description: A small stone boy mischievously turns his backside toward the city’s most famous water carrier.

name: ‘The Devil and the Hare’
description: Multiple versions of this horned sculpture, tied to local legend, have been stolen over the years.

name: Elf Freunde Kreisel
description: A team of concrete athletes stands in the middle of a traffic circle.

name: Dreiflüsseck
description: A park where the Inn and Ilz rivers join the Danube as it slinks off into Austria.

name: Deutsches Stuhlbaumuseum
description: Take a seat at this museum that celebrates a centuries-old tradition in a town known for its furniture.

name: Sendlinger Loch
description: An abandoned construction pit in Munich that turned into an accidental urban lake.

name: Lady Agnes
description: Visitors can tour this retired East German jetliner, and even get married inside.

name: Seiffen Christmas Clock
description: This clock in a village famous for toys gives a countdown to Christmas.

name: ‘Datenträger’ (‘Data Carrier’)
description: Rusted humanoid clutching a laptop and trailing cables.

name: Make Art Not War Mural
description: An important message and optimistic splash of color in a troubled neighborhood.

name: Rundfunkmuseum Cham
description: A museum of broadcasting with an array of vintage equipment and its own active transmitter.

name: The Golden Rift
description: A thin golden line commemorates the deadliest terrorist attack in recent German history.

name: Hans Albers Monument Reliefs
description: Carved scenes of sex work and unglamorous labour beneath a cultural hero.

name: Friedrich Fröbel Museum
description: The world's first kindergarten.

name: Petermännchen Statue at Schwerin Castle
description: A carved figure embodying the palace’s pint-sized but protective spirit rooted in local folklore.

name: 'Gesellschaftsspiegel' ('Society Mirror')
description: Elevated brass pavilions form a kaleidoscope of fragmented views of the surrounding historic cityscape.

name: Tank Monument Kleinmachnow
description: A truly obscure, hidden Cold War monument.

name: Local History Museum of Treuenbrietzen
description: Explore Treuenbrietzen’s past inside a restored medieval chapel with artifacts, crafts, and a historic pharmacy.

name: The Thumb
description: A monumental golden thumb rises as an iconic homage to Nouveau Réalisme, bold in scale and unapologetic in spirit.

name: Gildehaus Reformed Church
description: A church with a detached tower, believed to have been cast aside by the devil himself.

name: Grave of Marinus van der Lubbe
description: Ninety years after his execution, the man accused of starting the Reichstag fire was given a final resting place.

name: Memorial to the Children of Bullenhuser Damm
description: A suburban monument preserves the faces of twenty murdered Jewish boys and girls.

name: Johannisburg Palace Cork Model Collection
description: The world’s largest collection of cork models of ancient Roman buildings, made by a court baker and his son.

name: Stalin Villa
description: The home of Joseph Stalin during the Potsdam conference.

name: The Ice Age Erratics of Füssen
description: Two unremarkable-looking rocks on a cobblestone street are actually ancient wanderers; one a 150-million-year-old limestone, the other a 1.8-billion-year-old granite.

name: Nazi-Era Italian Laborers Memorial
description: A quiet pine forest near Nichel hides a powerful memorial to victims of a nearly forgotten war crime.

name: Waitzstraße
description: Dubbed Germany’s most accident-prone shopping street, this short stretch has seen around 30 cars crash into storefronts.

name: Hamburger SV Fan Burial Ground
description: Europe’s first football club cemetery, where lifelong supporters remain close to their team even after death.

name: Altwarengeschäft Remlinger
description: A second hand shop that enabled the plan of Germany's most iconic conman.

name: Garnisonkirche
description: Historic German church that was reconstructed and opened to the public in 2024.

name: Trafo-Haus Travemünder Allee
description: Abandoned transformer house on a country road tells of a time when art and politics were closely linked.

name: Spreepark
description: An eerie, derelict amusement park in Berlin.

name: The Return of the Cows
description: Art and a pun, all in one.

name: AquaDom
description: The largest free-standing aquarium in the world resides in a hotel lobby in Berlin.

name: Weissensee Abandoned Children's Hospital
description: A state-of-the-art medical facility from the Imperial era is now a derelict ruin.

name: Peristal Singum
description: Fall down the rabbit hole into a surreal journey of human consciousness "from ass to soul."

name: Ballhaus Riviera
description: The music is over at the Ballhaus Riviera Ballroom, but the ballroom remains in haunting disrepair.

name: Hubertusbad
description: An abandoned swimming pool decays on the outskirts of Berlin.

name: Hitler's Olympic Village
description: The 1936 Summer Olympics site is a crumbling remnant of sportsmanship and a history its host country would like to forget.

name: Deutsches Currywurst Museum
description: A museum dedicated to a Cold War era sausage.

name: Pinakothek der Moderne Futuro House
description: One of the most easily accessible examples of the rare 1960s UFO houses.

name: Arthouse Tacheles
description: An old department store turned Nazi prison turned artist commune.

name: Böhmisches Brauhaus
description: The abandoned remains of this Victorian brewery hide crumbling bowling alleys and rusting industrial gear.

name: Abandoned Airfield Rangsdorf
description: The ruined remains of the airport Claus von Stauffenberg departed in his failed attempt to kill Adolf Hitler.

name: Prora
description: Hitler's bleak vision of relaxation still stands as a mostly abandoned 10,000-room resort complex.

name: Propeller Island City Lodge
description: Come stay at a live-in art installation.

name: Off-World Gaming
description: A dilapidated space station with overtones of 'Alien' and 'Bladerunner' featuring a multiplayer games galore.

name: Abandoned Crematorium and Cemetery
description: The ruins of an abandoned crematorium haunt an eerie deserted German cemetery.

name: touched echo
description: Silent memorial, using bone conduction to take people back to the Dresden Bombing of 1945.

name: Zucker-Museum
description: A sweet museum focused on beets.

name: Senfmuseum (Mustard Museum)
description: This small condiment museum makes all manner of mustards with a 200-year-old mill.

name: Wunderkammer Olbricht at me Collectors Room
description: A remarkable cabinet of curiosities in an art space in the center of Berlin.

name: Abandoned SS Brotfabrik
description: A crumbling bakery where forced laborers from the nearby concentration camp toiled to feed their fellow prisoners and Nazi soldiers.

name: Listening to the Future
description: Residing along a busy thoroughfare, this sculpture urges passersby to consider the future.

name: Ost-West-Friedenskirche (East-West Peace Church)
description: An Orthodox church inside a small "Garden of Eden" built by a man who had a vision.

name: Unihase
description: Inside this discotheque resides a unique memorial dedicated to one club-goers favorite pet.

name: Bad Dürkheim Aerial Tramway
description: Ruins of an aerial tramway that ran for only eight years.

name: Düsseldorf-Reisholz Rhine Powerline Crossing
description: An electrical pylon under whose legs a normal gauge train can run.

name: Henninger Turm
description: The world's only silo that was topped by two revolving restaurants has unfortunately been demolished.

name: The Heusweiler Motorway Faraday Cage
description: A Faraday Cage protects cars from a nearby high-power transmitter.

name: Speyer Wine Bottle
description: Aged nearly 1,700 years, the world's oldest bottle of wine is probably still drinkable.
